name: validator

metadata.notes: Validator strictly enforces input format, range constraints (x, q <= 4e5, a_i <= 1e9, K <= 1e9), and operation count. Checker uses robust tokenization to tolerate trailing whitespace/newlines, resolving previous 'checker_rejects_standard_output' issues. For YES cases, it verifies slot range, team/slot uniqueness, and modulo constraints. For NO cases, it verifies interval bounds and Hall violation count using an O(1) residue-range intersection check per team, avoiding O(N) brute-force over large K. Lexicographical minimality is verified for small K; for large K, violation validity is strictly checked while trusting solver optimality to prevent TLE. Test generator covers all specified buckets deterministically, includes samples, and marks oracle expectations based on scale. All tools are stateless, dependency-free, and strictly follow the checker judge type contract.
